search_query: how to reply to cheers or ta
combined_text: "dear british people: what is the proper response to ""cheers"" or ""ta""?" "i'm an american living in america but a lot of the customers at my workplace seem to hail from british english-speaking parts of the world and will say ""cheers"" or ""ta"" instead of ""thanks."" ""you're welcome"" seems like a strange response to either of those acknowledgments. what do you say/do
